apsNote: Ga-Rankuwa and eMalahleni campuses may have separate intakes.
requiredDocuments: Certified copy of South African ID or valid passport
Certified copy of matric certificate or statement of results
Proof of residence (not older than 3 months)

internationalStudents.note: International applicants require SAQA evaluation of qualifications.

applicationCloses.note: CORRECTION from 31 Jul; tiered: high-demand 15 Jun, many 31 Jul, general 30 Sep

apsSystem: APS /42 (7-point NSC rating scale; Life Orientation included for most programmes)

flags: feeIntl: international application fee not separately confirmed on official tut.ac.za — R240 appears to apply to all first-time applicants; international students additionally pay an annual levy of R1,820
healthSciencesCloses: 15 June 2026 confirmed for Nursing and Radiography from programme-closing-dates page; Pharmacy listed as 30 Sept 2026

APS values for several programmes sourced from third-party sites (varsitywise.com, coursematch.co.za) cross-referenced but not directly read from the official 2027 PDF (binary-encoded, not machine-readable)
Tshwane School for Business and Society — faculty listed on official undergraduate page but no programmes with APS confirmed from official source
prospectusUrl is the official 2027 First Year Course Information PDF on tut.ac.za; the traditional PART prospectus series (Part 1–9) is the 2026 series
